Empires & Puzzles Andy Jay Review: The Blazing DJ Hero Guide

Andy Jay brings the heat to the beach party with a massive 450% hit to all enemies and a unique dance mechanic. But does his Slow mana speed kill the vibe? We break down his stats, skills, and why he might be the king of Rush Wars.
Andy Jay Review: Is This DJ Worth The Spin?
Andy Jay: The Blazing DJ That Spins Enemies Round and Round
Andy Jay arrived in June 2025 as part of the Beach Party event, bringing a fresh beat to the Fire roster. As a

, he’s not just here to play music; he’s here to drop the bass—and by bass, we mean a massive 450% damage nuke on the entire enemy team. He introduces the "Blazing Rave Dance" mechanic, which functions similarly to Guardian Gazelle’s dance but with a defensive twist. While his Slow mana speed might make speed demons cringe, his raw power and unique utility make him a fascinating hero to analyze.

Andy Jay is undeniably cool. He’s decked out in full DJ gear, literally wearing a turntable as part of his outfit (or is it a magical construct? Who knows!). The vibrant colors, the sunglasses, and the sheer energy radiating from his card art scream "Summer Party." Unlike the gritty, battle-hardened warriors of Season 1, Andy is here to have a good time. It’s a refreshing, fun design that perfectly fits the Beach Party theme. He looks like he’s about to drop the hottest mixtape in Corellia.

, Andy Jay’s stats were top-tier upon release in mid-2025. However, looking at the latest heroes from late 2025 (like the Magic Tower and Construct heroes pushing 1300+ Attack and 1300+ Defense), Andy has naturally slipped slightly behind the absolute cutting edge.
1229 is still very high, ensuring his special skill hits like a truck.
1194 is solid enough to help him survive until he fires, especially with his high health pool.
2193 is excellent, giving him the durability needed for a Slow mana hero.
He is by no means "outdated," but he is no longer the stat king he might have been 6 months ago. He sits comfortably in the "Modern & Strong" category.

enemies. This is one of the hardest-hitting AoE skills in the game. Most "Hit All" heroes at Slow speed hover around 350-400% or have conditional damage. Andy just slaps everyone for 450%.
When the dance starts, it removes
existing status effects from your allies (buffs and ailments). This acts as a mega-cleanse.
Allies dancing are immune to new status effects. No poison, no defense down, no mind control.
Unlike Gazelle, whose dance refreshes on her own turn, Andy's dance extends when
dancing allies are hit by special skills
. This makes him punishing to attack into.

Just like Gazelle, if Andy dies, the dance ends. Also, because the dance prevents new status effects, you cannot buff your team with Attack Up or other boons while dancing. You trade versatility for raw defense and immunity.
With a level 29 Magic/Styx troop (+20%) and the +4% Sorcerer talent, you can get him to 10 tiles.

+50% Defense is mathematically one of the strongest buffs in the game for survival, significantly reducing incoming damage.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

bonus offers damage reduction from DoT (Damage over Time) effects. It ranges from -20% to -60% depending on the number of heroes. It's a nice passive mitigation, especially against burn/poison heavy teams, but it's not a game-changer that forces you to run a full family team.
Adds a bit of extra chip damage (around 292 over 2 turns). It’s free damage, so we take it, but it won’t decide the match.
Highly situational. Useful against other Beach Party heroes or select others like costumed Thorne, but generally a flavor passive.

for a Slow hero. It guarantees he won't be silenced, mana-blocked, or defense-downed before he gets a chance to charge. It virtually ensures he will fire his special at least once in many battles.
When released, Andy was immediately identified as a Rush War monster. His similarity to Guardian Gazelle (a fan favorite) but with an offensive twist made him highly desirable.
While his stats are slightly lower than the December 2025 wave, his 450% damage multiplier is timeless. High percentage multipliers age much better than flat damage or low-percentage hits.
He is too slow for the top-100 defense meta, where speed is king.

Excellent for map stages and towers where you can use mana potions to charge him up and wipe entire waves.
Maximizes his survival. Since the dance ends if he dies, keeping him alive is priority #1.

Leans into his 450% damage. At that multiplier, every point of Attack adds significant damage. He already has good bulk, and the Aether Power protects him early.

path is recommended. 450% to all is a finisher move. You want that hit to wipe the board, not just tickle them. His base defensive stats are high enough to survive, especially with the Ailment Immunity start.
Removes all ailments from allies instantly.
+50% defense and immunity to new ailments makes the team incredibly hard to kill while dancing.
Solves the biggest weakness of Slow heroes (getting controlled before firing).
Without Rush rules or mana acceleration, 12 tiles is an eternity in modern E&P.
If he dies, the buffs vanish, leaving your team vulnerable.
While dancing, your team cannot receive other buffs (like Attack Up). This limits synergy with other buffers.
Unlike Gazelle who reduces damage taken, Andy relies solely on the defense buff. He doesn't heal himself.
This is his home. He is arguably one of the top 5 Rush tanks/flanks in the game.
High damage to all clears waves efficiently.
Great for farming and clearing hard bosses.
Andy Jay demands a team built around him. You don't put him in a team that relies on stacking ten different buffs, because he will wipe them all out.
Ludwig charges everyone. Andy wipes the board and protects the team. Alfrike controls anyone left standing.

Minion summoners work great with Andy. The minions provide a meatshield while he charges, and his dance protects the summoners so they can keep churning out minions. The dance doesn't prevent minions from spawning.
it cannot be dispelled, killing Andy removes it. Snipers who can one-shot him are his biggest threat.
Reducing his mana keeps him from ever starting the party.
Don't pair him with Black Knight or generic Attack buffers. He will wipe their buffs when he starts dancing.
If a Taunt hero is dancing, they keep the Taunt (if it's a minion/passive) but if it's a buff, it gets wiped!

If you pulled Andy Jay early, congratulations! He will carry you through the entire map. His damage is high enough to one-shot almost any map wave. Focus on keeping him alive with healers. Don't worry about the "Slow" speed in map stages; you can ghost tiles to charge him.

He is a luxury tool for your Rush War roster. Is he worth the Alpha Aethers? If you take Rush Wars seriously, yes. The ailment immunity is critical. Outside of Rush, he’s a fun toy but likely won't displace your fast 2025 meta heroes.
The community reaction was mixed but generally positive about his damage.
"450% damage to all is pretty big plus he makes the rest of the team immune to status ailments while giving a pretty big defense buff... I pulled him and I’m happy."
Many users compared him to Guardian Gazelle, noting the trade-off:
not even slow speed... he feels incredibly underwhelming."
One common complaint was the dance duration:
"It’s already slow with 3 turns, it needs as much help as it could."

Andy Jay is the life of the party—specifically the Rush Attack party. He is a high-risk, high-reward hero who brings nuclear damage and a unique defensive mechanic. While he sits on the bench for standard raids due to his speed, when the conditions are right (Rush), he is an absolute superstar.
A specialized powerhouse. Essential for Rush enthusiasts, a fun nuke for everyone else.
A comprehensive review of Andy Jay, the 5-star Fire hero from the Beach Party family. Analysis of his stats, Blazing Rave Dance skill, and best uses in Rush Wars and PvP.
